The bat virus HKU5-CoV-2 was newly discovered and described in a February 2025 paper. While it is capable of entering cells via the human ACE2 receptor in laboratory settings, researchers emphasize that it infects human cells much less efficiently than SARS-CoV-2 and that there are currently no known human cases. Searches as recent as April/May 2025 show only further phylogenetic or genetic analyses of the virus, with no indication of known or suspected human infections or outbreaks. Given the very short remaining time window (mid-May 2025 to July 1, 2025), the base rate of an isolated, newly discovered wild bat virus independently spilling over into humans, or a lab-acquired infection occurring and being reported within just six weeks, is exceptionally low. I am setting the probability at the minimum allowed threshold of 5%.
